rawText:
The artificial intelligence industry has entered a phase of extraordinary turbulence. Three overlapping battles are being fought simultaneously: a hardware war over who controls the chips that power AI systems, a financial war over who can raise enough capital to survive, and a strategic war over who will dominate the technology for decades to come. Each contest feeds the others, creating a volatile landscape where trillion-dollar valuations coexist with deep uncertainty about whether anyone will turn a profit anytime soon.

At the center of the hardware struggle sits the company that has profited most from the AI boom. Having built a near-monopoly in the specialized processors that train and run AI models, this dominant chipmaker generates revenue on a scale that seemed unimaginable just three years ago. Yet its position is under threat from multiple directions. Its largest customers are designing rival chips that can cut costs in half. Startups are raising record sums to build alternatives. And export restrictions have opened the door for competitors in markets once out of reach. In response, the chipmaker is transforming itself into a vertically integrated platform, selling not just processors but networking equipment, software models, and complete data-center systems. The goal is to become so embedded across the AI supply chain that switching away becomes impractical.

Meanwhile, the leading AI laboratories are locked in a fierce race to go public. Three firms are preparing initial public offerings that would collectively dwarf the total amount raised through American listings over the past decade. Each lab is burning through cash at a staggering pace, pouring billions into infrastructure while remaining far from profitability. One has forecast that it will not generate positive cash flow until 2030, even after raising over a hundred billion dollars in private funding. The rivalry among these labs has turned personal, with lawsuits, government contract disputes, and public attacks becoming routine tactics. Their competition for defense contracts and enterprise customers is intensifying as their products converge in capability.

Investors, for their part, are struggling to make sense of it all. Historical analysis shows that financial markets are remarkably poor at predicting which industries will be disrupted by new technologies and which will adapt. Share prices of companies deemed most vulnerable to AI have fallen sharply, yet bond markets have barely reacted, suggesting the broader economy may not feel the impact for years. Academic research confirms that analysts tend to overestimate the profitability of firms whose technology is becoming outdated, while simultaneously underestimating how effectively incumbents can reinvent themselves. In the background, one established technology giant with deep pockets and massive existing profits is quietly funding its own AI ambitions without the drama of fundraising or public feuds.

The AI industry's future remains genuinely uncertain. The technology continues to advance unevenly, excelling at some tasks while stalling on others. Nobody knows whether today's enormous infrastructure investments will yield returns or become expensive monuments to misplaced optimism. What is clear is that the struggle for control over artificial intelligence has become one of the most consequential business battles in modern history, with stakes that have few historical parallels.

overview.title: The AI Power Struggle: Chips, Cash, and Boundless Ambition

sentences.original: The artificial intelligence industry has entered a phase of extraordinary turbulence.
sentences.translation: 인공지능 산업이 전례 없는 격동의 국면에 접어들었다.

vocabulary.example: The financial markets experienced severe turbulence after the announcement.
The company achieved extraordinary growth in just two years.

expressions.usage: 어떤 분야가 새로운 시기나 단계로 진입했음을 나타내는 격식체 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] has entered a phase of [adjective] [noun]
patterns.explanation: 어떤 주체가 새로운 국면에 진입했음을 현재완료로 선언하는 도입 구조
examples: The economy has entered a phase of sustained recovery.
The relationship between the two nations has entered a phase of cautious cooperation.

drillQuestions.question: The AI industry has entered a phase of extraordinary ______.

sentences.notes: 기사의 도입문으로 전체 논지를 한 문장으로 선언한다. entered a phase of라는 표현은 새 시대의 개막을 알리는 저널리즘적 관용구이다.

sentences.original: Three overlapping battles are being fought simultaneously: a hardware war over who controls the chips that power AI systems, a financial war over who can raise enough capital to survive, and a strategic war over who will dominate the technology for decades to come.
sentences.translation: 세 가지 전투가 동시에 겹쳐서 벌어지고 있다: AI 시스템을 구동하는 칩을 누가 지배하느냐를 둘러싼 하드웨어 전쟁, 생존을 위해 충분한 자본을 누가 조달할 수 있느냐를 둘러싼 금융 전쟁, 그리고 향후 수십 년간 이 기술을 누가 지배할 것인가를 둘러싼 전략 전쟁이다.

grammarElements.text: a hardware war ... a financial war ... and a strategic war
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 동격 나열 (3항 병렬)

vocabulary.example: The departments had overlapping responsibilities that caused confusion.
The company is pursuing growth in three markets simultaneously.
The startup struggled to raise enough capital to scale its operations.

expressions.usage: 장기적 시간 범위를 강조할 때 사용하는 표현으로, 미래의 지속성을 부각한다

patterns.pattern: [Number] [battles/challenges] are being fought simultaneously: [A], [B], and [C]
patterns.explanation: 여러 전투/도전을 콜론 뒤에 3항 병렬로 열거하여 상황의 복잡성을 구조적으로 보여주는 패턴
examples: Three crises are unfolding simultaneously: a banking panic, a currency collapse, and a political standoff.
Two transformations are being driven simultaneously: a shift to renewable energy and a restructuring of the labor market.

drillQuestions.question: Three ______ battles are being fought simultaneously.

sentences.notes: 콜론(:) 뒤에 세 항목을 병렬로 나열하는 구조는 영어 분석 기사의 핵심 기법이다. who + 동사 간접의문문이 각 항목에 반복되어 리듬감을 만든다.

koreanAlignment.en: a hardware war over who controls

sentences.original: Each contest feeds the others, creating a volatile landscape where trillion-dollar valuations coexist with deep uncertainty about whether anyone will turn a profit anytime soon.
sentences.translation: 각 경쟁은 다른 경쟁을 부추기며, 수조 달러의 기업 가치와 누군가가 가까운 시일 내에 수익을 낼 수 있을지에 대한 깊은 불확실성이 공존하는 불안정한 지형을 만들어내고 있다.

grammarElements.text: where trillion-dollar valuations coexist with deep uncertainty
grammarElements.label: 관계부사절

vocabulary.example: The stock market has been extremely volatile in recent weeks.
Tech valuations soared during the pandemic-era boom.
Extreme wealth and poverty coexist in many major cities.

expressions.usage: 사업이 손익분기점을 넘어 실질적 이익을 내기 시작할 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: Each [element] feeds the others, creating [result] where [X] coexist(s) with [Y]
patterns.explanation: 각 요소가 상호 작용하며 모순적 요소들이 공존하는 상황을 만들어내는 구조
examples: Each policy feeds the others, creating an environment where innovation coexists with regulation.
Each crisis feeds the others, creating a cycle where hope coexists with desperation.

drillQuestions.question: Trillion-dollar valuations ______ with deep uncertainty.

sentences.notes: feeds the others라는 은유가 경쟁의 상호 강화 메커니즘을 생동감 있게 전달한다. turn a profit은 비즈니스 기사의 핵심 관용구.

sentences.original: At the center of the hardware struggle sits the company that has profited most from the AI boom.
sentences.translation: 하드웨어 전쟁의 중심에는 AI 붐에서 가장 큰 이익을 거둔 기업이 자리 잡고 있다.

grammarElements.text: At the center of the hardware struggle
grammarElements.label: 장소 부사구 (도치 구문)

grammarElements.text: the company that has profited most from the AI boom
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계절 포함)

vocabulary.example: The firm profited enormously from the shift to remote work.
The housing boom attracted speculators from across the country.

expressions.usage: 어떤 사안이나 논쟁의 핵심에 있음을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: At the center of [struggle/debate] sits [subject]
patterns.explanation: 장소 부사구를 문두에 놓고 주어와 동사를 도치하여 극적으로 핵심 주체를 소개하는 구조
examples: At the center of the trade dispute sits a single commodity: semiconductors.
At the center of the controversy sits the CEO who approved the deal.

drillQuestions.question: At the center of the hardware struggle ______ the company that has profited most.

sentences.notes: 장소 부사구 도치(At the center ... sits the company)는 저널리즘에서 핵심 주체를 극적으로 소개하는 기법. 일반 어순은 The company sits at the center이다.

koreanAlignment.en: At the center of the hardware struggle
the company that has profited most

pronunciationNotes: profited [ˈprɒfɪtɪd] -- '프로피티드'로 첫 음절 강세, profit의 과거형

sentences.original: Having built a near-monopoly in the specialized processors that train and run AI models, this dominant chipmaker generates revenue on a scale that seemed unimaginable just three years ago.
sentences.translation: AI 모델을 훈련하고 구동하는 특수 프로세서에서 거의 독점적 지위를 구축한 이 지배적인 칩 제조업체는 불과 3년 전에는 상상할 수 없었던 규모의 매출을 올리고 있다.

grammarElements.text: Having built a near-monopoly in the specialized processors that train and run AI models
grammarElements.label: 완료 분사구문 (원인/배경)

grammarElements.text: revenue on a scale that seemed unimaginable just three years ago
grammarElements.label: 관계절을 포함한 목적어

vocabulary.example: The platform enjoys a near-monopoly in the mobile operating system market.
The company remains the dominant player in cloud computing.
The scale of destruction was unimaginable to most observers.

expressions.usage: 규모의 크기를 관계절로 수식하여 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: Having [past participle] [achievement], [subject] [present verb] [result]
patterns.explanation: 완료 분사구문으로 과거의 성취를 배경으로 제시한 뒤, 현재의 결과를 서술하는 구조
examples: Having secured exclusive contracts with major suppliers, the retailer dominates the market.
Having invested early in renewable energy, the utility now generates most of its power from wind.

drillQuestions.question: ______ built a near-monopoly, this chipmaker generates enormous revenue.

sentences.notes: Having built으로 시작하는 완료 분사구문이 현재의 매출 규모가 과거 독점 구축의 결과임을 인과적으로 연결한다. 문장이 길지만 구조는 명확하다.

koreanAlignment.en: on a scale that seemed unimaginable

sentences.original: Yet its position is under threat from multiple directions.
sentences.translation: 그러나 그 지위는 여러 방향에서 위협받고 있다.

vocabulary.example: Several endangered species are under threat from habitat loss.

expressions.usage: 위협이나 도전이 한 곳이 아닌 여러 곳에서 동시에 오고 있음을 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: Yet [possessive] position is under threat from [source]
patterns.explanation: 앞서 언급한 강점에 역접으로 전환하여 위협을 제시하는 전환 구조
examples: Yet its dominance is under threat from cheaper alternatives.
Yet their lead is under threat from fast-moving competitors.

drillQuestions.question: Yet its position is ______ threat from multiple directions.

sentences.notes: 짧지만 구조적으로 핵심적인 전환 문장. Yet이 이전 문장의 '상상 불가 매출'에서 '위협'으로 논의를 급전환시킨다.

sentences.original: Its largest customers are designing rival chips that can cut costs in half.
sentences.translation: 최대 고객사들이 비용을 절반으로 줄일 수 있는 경쟁 칩을 설계하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: rival chips that can cut costs in half
grammarElements.label: 관계절을 포함한 목적어

vocabulary.example: The two rival firms competed fiercely for the government contract.
The new manufacturing process could cut costs in half.

expressions.usage: 수량이나 비용을 반으로 줄이거나 나눌 때 사용하는 간결한 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject]'s largest customers are designing [alternatives] that can [advantage]
patterns.explanation: 가장 큰 고객이 오히려 경쟁 제품을 만들고 있다는 역설적 위협을 제시하는 구조
examples: The platform's largest advertisers are building in-house tools that can bypass it entirely.
The supplier's largest buyers are developing substitutes that can reduce dependence.

drillQuestions.question: Its largest customers are designing ______ chips that can cut costs in half.

sentences.notes: 고객이 곧 경쟁자가 된다는 아이러니가 핵심. 이 문장부터 세 문장 연속으로 위협의 출처를 나열하는 구조가 시작된다.

sentences.original: Startups are raising record sums to build alternatives.
sentences.translation: 스타트업들은 대안을 구축하기 위해 사상 최대 규모의 자금을 조달하고 있다.

vocabulary.example: Investors poured record sums into clean energy startups last year.

expressions.usage: 투자 유치 금액이 역대 최고 수준임을 강조하는 비즈니스 표현

patterns.pattern: [New entrants] are raising [unprecedented amounts] to [goal]
patterns.explanation: 신규 진입자가 전례 없는 자금을 조달해 도전하고 있음을 보여주는 패턴
examples: Challenger banks are raising record sums to compete with established lenders.
Biotech firms are raising unprecedented capital to develop next-generation therapies.

drillQuestions.question: Startups are raising ______ sums to build alternatives.

sentences.notes: 세 위협 중 두 번째. 짧고 간결한 문장으로 스타트업 위협을 압축적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.ko: 사상 최대 규모의 자금을 조달하고 있다

sentences.original: And export restrictions have opened the door for competitors in markets once out of reach.
sentences.translation: 그리고 수출 규제가 한때 접근이 불가능했던 시장에서 경쟁자들에게 문을 열어주었다.

grammarElements.text: in markets once out of reach
grammarElements.label: 장소 부사구 (축약 관계절)

vocabulary.example: The government imposed export restrictions on advanced semiconductor equipment.
Affordable housing remains out of reach for many young families.

expressions.usage: 새로운 기회나 가능성이 생겼음을 은유적으로 표현하는 관용구

patterns.pattern: [Policy/Event] has opened the door for [beneficiary] in [area]
patterns.explanation: 특정 정책이나 사건이 의도치 않게 새로운 기회를 만들어준 상황을 표현하는 패턴
examples: The regulatory change has opened the door for fintech companies in traditional banking.
The ban has opened the door for domestic manufacturers in sectors long dominated by imports.

drillQuestions.question: Export restrictions have opened the ______ for competitors.

sentences.notes: 세 위협 중 마지막. And로 시작하는 문장은 격식 문체에서 드문데, 여기서는 세 번째 위협을 추가하는 극적 효과를 위해 의도적으로 사용되었다.

sentences.original: In response, the chipmaker is transforming itself into a vertically integrated platform, selling not just processors but networking equipment, software models, and complete data-center systems.
sentences.translation: 이에 대응하여, 이 칩 제조업체는 프로세서뿐만 아니라 네트워킹 장비, 소프트웨어 모델, 완전한 데이터센터 시스템까지 판매하는 수직 통합 플랫폼으로 스스로를 변모시키고 있다.

grammarElements.text: itself into a vertically integrated platform
grammarElements.label: 재귀목적어 + 전치사구

grammarElements.text: selling not just processors but networking equipment, software models, and complete data-center systems
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (구체적 설명)

vocabulary.example: The company pursued a vertically integrated strategy, controlling everything from raw materials to retail.
The CEO is transforming the company from a hardware maker into a services provider.

expressions.usage: 범위의 확장을 강조하는 상관접속사 구조. not only ... but also와 동일한 의미

patterns.pattern: In response, [subject] is transforming itself into [new form], [verb-ing] not just [A] but [B], [C], and [D]
patterns.explanation: 위협에 대한 전략적 대응으로 사업 모델을 전환하고 있음을 제시하는 구조
examples: In response, the retailer is transforming itself into a logistics platform, offering not just products but warehousing, delivery, and data analytics.
In response, the bank is transforming itself into a fintech hub, providing not just loans but payment processing, insurance, and investment tools.

drillQuestions.question: The chipmaker is transforming itself into a ______ integrated platform.

sentences.notes: vertically integrated는 경영학/경제학의 핵심 개념으로, 공급망의 여러 단계를 하나의 기업이 통제하는 전략을 의미한다.

sentences.original: The goal is to become so embedded across the AI supply chain that switching away becomes impractical.
sentences.translation: 목표는 AI 공급망 전반에 너무나 깊이 자리 잡아 이탈하는 것이 비현실적이 되도록 하는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: to become so embedded ... that switching away becomes impractical
grammarElements.label: 보어 (to 부정사 + so ... that 구문)

vocabulary.example: The software is so deeply embedded in operations that replacing it would take years.
Switching to a new system at this stage would be impractical.

expressions.usage: 기존 제품이나 서비스에서 다른 대안으로 옮기는 행위를 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: The goal is to become so [adjective] that [consequence]
patterns.explanation: so ... that 결과 구문을 활용해 전략적 목표를 선명하게 제시하는 패턴
examples: The goal is to become so essential that no competitor can displace it.
The goal is to become so integrated into daily life that users cannot imagine alternatives.

drillQuestions.question: The goal is to become so ______ that switching away becomes impractical.

sentences.notes: so ... that 결과 구문이 '잠금 효과(lock-in effect)'라는 비즈니스 전략을 한 문장으로 압축한다. switching away는 고객 이탈을 의미하는 비즈니스 핵심 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: so embedded across the AI supply chain
koreanAlignment.ko: AI 공급망 전반에 너무나 깊이 자리 잡아

sentences.original: Meanwhile, the leading AI laboratories are locked in a fierce race to go public.
sentences.translation: 한편, 주요 AI 연구소들은 기업공개를 향한 치열한 경쟁에 갇혀 있다.

grammarElements.text: a fierce race to go public
grammarElements.label: 전치사 목적어 (to 부정사 수식)

vocabulary.example: The two companies are engaged in a fierce battle for market share.
The company plans to go public by the end of the year.

expressions.usage: 참여자들이 빠져나올 수 없는 격렬한 경쟁 상황을 묘사하는 표현

patterns.pattern: Meanwhile, [subject] are locked in [competition] to [goal]
patterns.explanation: 장면을 전환하면서 다른 주체들이 치열한 경쟁에 빠져 있음을 소개하는 구조
examples: Meanwhile, the major airlines are locked in a fierce race to dominate transatlantic routes.
Meanwhile, the pharmaceutical giants are locked in a bitter fight to control the vaccine supply.

drillQuestions.question: The leading AI laboratories are ______ in a fierce race to go public.

sentences.notes: Meanwhile로 하드웨어 전쟁에서 금융 전쟁으로 장면을 전환한다. locked in은 선택의 여지 없이 경쟁에 갇혀 있다는 뉘앙스.

koreanAlignment.en: are locked in a fierce race

sentences.original: Three firms are preparing initial public offerings that would collectively dwarf the total amount raised through American listings over the past decade.
sentences.translation: 세 기업이 미국에서 지난 10년간 상장을 통해 조달된 총액을 합산해도 왜소하게 만들 규모의 기업공개를 준비하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: initial public offerings that would collectively dwarf the total amount raised through American listings over the past decade
grammarElements.label: 관계절을 포함한 목적어

vocabulary.example: The new skyscraper dwarfs every other building in the city.
The company's initial public offering raised over ten billion dollars.
The three deals would collectively be worth over fifty billion dollars.

expressions.usage: 비교의 시간 범위를 설정하여 규모의 대조를 극대화하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] are preparing [offerings] that would collectively dwarf [comparison benchmark]
patterns.explanation: 준비 중인 것의 규모가 기존 기준을 압도함을 관계절로 강조하는 패턴
examples: The planned investments would collectively dwarf all previous spending on infrastructure.
Their combined revenues would collectively dwarf the GDP of most small nations.

drillQuestions.question: Three firms are preparing IPOs that would collectively ______ the total raised over the past decade.

sentences.notes: dwarf를 동사로 사용하는 것이 핵심. '난쟁이처럼 보이게 만든다'는 원래 의미에서 '규모로 압도하다'라는 비유적 의미로 확장되었다.
pronunciationNotes: dwarf [dwɔːrf] -- '드워프'로 한 음절, dw- 자음군 주의

sentences.original: Each lab is burning through cash at a staggering pace, pouring billions into infrastructure while remaining far from profitability.
sentences.translation: 각 연구소는 어마어마한 속도로 현금을 소진하며, 수십억 달러를 인프라에 쏟아붓고 있지만 수익성과는 여전히 거리가 멀다.

grammarElements.text: pouring billions into infrastructure while remaining far from profitability
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (동시 행위 + while 대조)

vocabulary.example: The startup was burning through its funding at an alarming rate.
The project required a staggering amount of investment.
The company has yet to achieve profitability despite rapid revenue growth.

expressions.usage: 아직 이익을 내기까지 갈 길이 멀다는 것을 완곡하게 표현하는 비즈니스 용어

patterns.pattern: [Subject] is burning through [resource] at a [adjective] pace, [verb-ing] while remaining far from [goal]
patterns.explanation: 빠른 속도로 자원을 소진하면서도 목표에는 미치지 못하는 모순적 상황을 묘사하는 패턴
examples: The government is burning through reserves at an alarming pace, spending heavily while remaining far from fiscal stability.
The company is burning through talent at a remarkable pace, hiring thousands while remaining far from operational efficiency.

drillQuestions.question: Each lab is burning ______ cash at a staggering pace.

sentences.notes: burn through는 현금 소진을 생생하게 표현하는 비즈니스 필수 구동사. while remaining은 동시에 진행되는 대조적 상황을 연결한다.

sentences.original: One has forecast that it will not generate positive cash flow until 2030, even after raising over a hundred billion dollars in private funding.
sentences.translation: 한 기업은 1,000억 달러 이상의 사모펀드를 조달하고도 2030년까지 양(+)의 현금흐름을 창출하지 못할 것이라고 전망했다.

grammarElements.text: that it will not generate positive cash flow until 2030
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 (목적어)

grammarElements.text: even after raising over a hundred billion dollars in private funding
grammarElements.label: 양보 부사구

vocabulary.example: Analysts forecast that revenue will decline by ten percent this quarter.
The company finally achieved positive cash flow in its fifth year of operation.

expressions.usage: 거액의 자금 조달에도 불구하고 여전히 부족하다는 역설을 강조하는 양보 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] has forecast that it will not [achieve goal] until [distant date], even after [enormous effort]
patterns.explanation: 막대한 노력에도 불구하고 목표 달성이 먼 미래임을 강조하는 양보 구조
examples: The airline has forecast that it will not return to pre-pandemic levels until 2028, even after cutting costs by a third.
The project has forecast that it will not break even until 2035, even after securing government subsidies.

drillQuestions.question: One has forecast that it will not generate positive ______ until 2030.

sentences.notes: a hundred billion dollars라는 구체적 숫자와 until 2030이라는 시점이 AI 업계의 막대한 투자 규모와 긴 수익 회수 기간을 충격적으로 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: will not generate positive cash flow
even after raising over a hundred billion dollars

pronunciationNotes: forecast [ˈfɔːrkæst] -- '포어캐스트'로 첫 음절 강세, 과거형도 forecast

sentences.original: The rivalry among these labs has turned personal, with lawsuits, government contract disputes, and public attacks becoming routine tactics.
sentences.translation: 이 연구소들 간의 경쟁은 개인적 수준으로 변질되었으며, 소송, 정부 계약 분쟁, 공개적 공격이 일상적인 전술이 되었다.

grammarElements.text: with lawsuits, government contract disputes, and public attacks becoming routine tactics
grammarElements.label: with + 명사 + 분사 (부대상황)

vocabulary.example: The rivalry between the two tech giants has intensified over the past year.
Background checks have become a routine part of the hiring process.

expressions.usage: 비즈니스 경쟁이 사적 감정이 개입된 수준으로 악화되었음을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Competition] has turned personal, with [A], [B], and [C] becoming routine tactics
patterns.explanation: 경쟁이 개인적 수준으로 악화되었음을 구체적 행위 나열로 입증하는 구조
examples: The dispute has turned personal, with smear campaigns, leaks, and boycotts becoming routine tactics.
The trade war has turned personal, with tariffs, sanctions, and diplomatic snubs becoming routine tactics.

drillQuestions.question: The rivalry has turned ______, with lawsuits becoming routine tactics.

sentences.notes: with + 명사 + becoming 구조가 '개인적으로 변질되었다'는 주장을 구체적 사례로 뒷받침하는 역할을 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: lawsuits, government contract disputes, and public attacks
koreanAlignment.ko: 소송, 정부 계약 분쟁, 공개적 공격

sentences.original: Their competition for defense contracts and enterprise customers is intensifying as their products converge in capability.
sentences.translation: 국방 계약과 기업 고객을 둘러싼 이들의 경쟁은 제품의 역량이 수렴하면서 더욱 심화되고 있다.

grammarElements.text: Their competition for defense contracts and enterprise customers
grammarElements.label: 주어 (전치사구 수식)

grammarElements.text: as their products converge in capability
grammarElements.label: as 이유절

vocabulary.example: Competition is intensifying as more players enter the market.
The technologies are converging to create a single unified platform.

expressions.usage: 경쟁 제품들의 기능 차이가 줄어들고 있음을 기술적으로 표현하는 용어

patterns.pattern: [Competition] is intensifying as [products/services] converge in [dimension]
patterns.explanation: 제품이 유사해질수록 경쟁이 격화된다는 인과 관계를 as절로 연결하는 패턴
examples: The battle for subscribers is intensifying as streaming platforms converge in content quality.
The fight for market share is intensifying as smartphones converge in features.

drillQuestions.question: Competition is intensifying as their products ______ in capability.

sentences.notes: converge in capability는 제품 차별화가 사라지고 있다는 의미로, 이것이 역설적으로 경쟁을 더 치열하게 만든다는 통찰을 담고 있다.

koreanAlignment.en: as their products converge in capability

sentences.original: Investors, for their part, are struggling to make sense of it all.
sentences.translation: 투자자들은 투자자들 나름대로 이 모든 상황을 이해하려고 고군분투하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: to make sense of it all
grammarElements.label: to 부정사 목적어

vocabulary.example: Many retailers are struggling to adapt to the shift toward online shopping.

expressions.usage: 다른 참여자의 시각으로 전환할 때 사용하는 삽입구
복잡하거나 혼란스러운 상황을 이해하려고 노력할 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Actors], for their part, are struggling to make sense of [complex situation]
patterns.explanation: 새로운 관점의 참여자를 소개하면서 상황의 복잡성을 강조하는 전환 구조
examples: Regulators, for their part, are struggling to make sense of the rapidly evolving landscape.
Consumers, for their part, are struggling to make sense of the conflicting information.

drillQuestions.question: Investors are struggling to make ______ of it all.

sentences.notes: for their part라는 삽입구가 칩 제조업체 → AI 연구소 → 투자자로 이어지는 세 번째 시각 전환을 자연스럽게 연결한다.

koreanAlignment.en: are struggling to make sense of it all
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 모든 것을 이해하려고 고군분투하고 있다

sentences.original: Historical analysis shows that financial markets are remarkably poor at predicting which industries will be disrupted by new technologies and which will adapt.
sentences.translation: 역사적 분석에 따르면, 금융 시장은 어떤 산업이 신기술에 의해 파괴될 것이고 어떤 산업이 적응할 것인지를 예측하는 데 현저히 서투르다.

grammarElements.text: that financial markets are remarkably poor at predicting ...
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 (목적어)

grammarElements.text: which industries will be disrupted ... and which will adapt
grammarElements.label: 간접의문문 (병렬)

vocabulary.example: The algorithm performed remarkably well on unseen data.
The taxi industry was disrupted by ride-hailing apps within just a few years.

expressions.usage: 기대와 달리 특정 능력이 매우 부족함을 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Evidence] shows that [subject] are remarkably poor at predicting which [A] and which [B]
patterns.explanation: 객관적 근거를 제시하면서 예측 능력의 한계를 which 병렬 구조로 보여주는 패턴
examples: Research shows that experts are remarkably poor at predicting which startups will succeed and which will fail.
Data shows that voters are remarkably poor at predicting which policies will help them and which will hurt.

drillQuestions.question: Financial markets are remarkably poor at ______ which industries will be disrupted.

sentences.notes: which ... and which 병렬 간접의문문이 예측의 두 가지 차원(파괴 vs 적응)을 대조적으로 제시한다. remarkably poor는 '놀랍도록 못한다'는 아이러니를 담고 있다.

sentences.original: Share prices of companies deemed most vulnerable to AI have fallen sharply, yet bond markets have barely reacted, suggesting the broader economy may not feel the impact for years.
sentences.translation: AI에 가장 취약하다고 간주되는 기업들의 주가는 급락했지만, 채권 시장은 거의 반응하지 않았으며, 이는 광범위한 경제가 수년간 그 영향을 체감하지 못할 수 있음을 시사한다.

grammarElements.text: Share prices of companies deemed most vulnerable to AI
grammarElements.label: 주어 (축약 관계절 수식)

grammarElements.text: yet bond markets have barely reacted
grammarElements.label: yet 역접절

grammarElements.text: suggesting the broader economy may not feel the impact for years
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결론)

vocabulary.example: The building was deemed unsafe and evacuated immediately.
Small businesses are particularly vulnerable to economic downturns.
The market barely moved despite the dramatic announcement.

expressions.usage: 데이터에서 도출된 함의를 분사구문으로 제시하는 분석적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Market A] has [reacted strongly], yet [Market B] has barely reacted, suggesting [implication]
patterns.explanation: 두 시장의 상반된 반응을 대조하고 그 함의를 도출하는 분석적 구조
examples: Tech stocks have surged, yet consumer spending has barely changed, suggesting the gains are not yet reaching households.
Credit spreads have widened, yet equity markets have barely noticed, suggesting investors are selectively pricing risk.

drillQuestions.question: Companies ______ most vulnerable to AI have seen share prices fall.

sentences.notes: 주가와 채권 시장의 상반된 반응이라는 시장 간 괴리(divergence)를 날카롭게 포착한 문장. deemed는 과거분사의 축약 관계절 용법.

koreanAlignment.en: Share prices of companies deemed most vulnerable
yet bond markets have barely reacted
koreanAlignment.ko: 그러나 채권 시장은 거의 반응하지 않았다

koreanAlignment.en: suggesting the broader economy may not feel the impact
koreanAlignment.ko: 광범위한 경제가 영향을 체감하지 못할 수 있음을 시사하며

sentences.original: Academic research confirms that analysts tend to overestimate the profitability of firms whose technology is becoming outdated, while simultaneously underestimating how effectively incumbents can reinvent themselves.
sentences.translation: 학술 연구에 따르면, 분석가들은 기술이 구식화되고 있는 기업의 수익성을 과대평가하는 동시에 기존 대기업들이 얼마나 효과적으로 스스로를 재창조할 수 있는지는 과소평가하는 경향이 있다.

grammarElements.text: while simultaneously underestimating how effectively incumbents can reinvent themselves
grammarElements.label: while 대조절

vocabulary.example: Investors consistently overestimate the growth potential of hot sectors.
New entrants often underestimate the ability of incumbents to adapt.
The company's technology is becoming outdated in the face of rapid innovation.

expressions.usage: 기업이 근본적으로 사업 모델이나 전략을 바꾸어 새롭게 거듭남을 표현하는 비즈니스 관용구

patterns.pattern: [Research] confirms that [actors] tend to overestimate [X], while simultaneously underestimating [Y]
patterns.explanation: 학술적 근거를 토대로 두 방향의 판단 오류를 대조하는 분석적 구조
examples: Studies confirm that managers tend to overestimate the risks of change, while simultaneously underestimating the cost of inaction.
Evidence confirms that policymakers tend to overestimate short-term costs, while simultaneously underestimating long-term benefits.

drillQuestions.question: Analysts tend to overestimate profitability while underestimating how effectively ______ can reinvent themselves.

sentences.notes: overestimate와 underestimate의 대조가 분석가들의 이중적 판단 오류를 정교하게 드러낸다. incumbents(기존 사업자)는 비즈니스 분석의 핵심 용어.

koreanAlignment.en: how effectively incumbents can reinvent themselves
koreanAlignment.ko: 기존 대기업들이 얼마나 효과적으로 스스로를 재창조할 수 있는지

sentences.original: In the background, one established technology giant with deep pockets and massive existing profits is quietly funding its own AI ambitions without the drama of fundraising or public feuds.
sentences.translation: 그 이면에서, 넉넉한 자금력과 막대한 기존 수익을 가진 한 확립된 기술 대기업이 자금 조달이나 공개적 다툼의 소란 없이 조용히 자체 AI 야심에 자금을 투입하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: one established technology giant with deep pockets and massive existing profits
grammarElements.label: 주어 (전치사구 수식)

grammarElements.text: without the drama of fundraising or public feuds
grammarElements.label: without 부사구 (대조)

vocabulary.example: Only companies with deep pockets can afford the infrastructure costs of AI development.
Corporate feuds between rival executives often spill into the public domain.
The company's AI ambitions require billions in annual investment.

expressions.usage: 관심의 초점 밖에서 조용히 무언가가 진행되고 있음을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: In the background, [subject] is quietly [verb-ing] [goal] without the drama of [alternative approaches]
patterns.explanation: 무대 뒤에서 조용히 그러나 효과적으로 행동하는 주체를 소개하는 대조적 구조
examples: In the background, the sovereign wealth fund is quietly building a portfolio without the drama of public bidding wars.
In the background, the conglomerate is quietly acquiring competitors without the drama of hostile takeovers.

drillQuestions.question: One established technology giant with ______ is quietly funding its AI ambitions.

sentences.notes: 다른 기업들의 소란스러운 경쟁과 대조되는 '조용한 강자'를 소개하는 문장. quietly와 without the drama가 대조의 핵심. deep pockets는 비즈니스 필수 관용어.

koreanAlignment.en: without the drama of fundraising or public feuds
koreanAlignment.ko: 자금 조달이나 공개적 다툼의 소란 없이

sentences.original: The AI industry's future remains genuinely uncertain.
sentences.translation: AI 산업의 미래는 진정으로 불확실한 상태로 남아 있다.

vocabulary.example: The outcome remains genuinely unclear even to insiders.

expressions.usage: 가식적 겸손이 아닌 실제 불확실성을 강조할 때 genuinely를 삽입하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject]'s future remains genuinely [adjective]
patterns.explanation: 어떤 주제의 미래가 여전히 불확실함을 선언하며 결론부를 여는 구조
examples: The peace process's future remains genuinely fragile.
The industry's trajectory remains genuinely unpredictable.

drillQuestions.question: The AI industry's future remains ______ uncertain.

sentences.notes: 마지막 문단의 도입문. genuinely는 수사적 겸양이 아닌 실질적 불확실성을 강조하는 부사로, 필자의 솔직한 평가를 담고 있다.

sentences.original: The technology continues to advance unevenly, excelling at some tasks while stalling on others.
sentences.translation: 이 기술은 일부 작업에서는 뛰어나면서도 다른 작업에서는 정체하는 등 불균등하게 계속 발전하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: excelling at some tasks while stalling on others
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (대조적 동시 행위)

vocabulary.example: Economic recovery has progressed unevenly across different regions.
The model excels at pattern recognition but struggles with reasoning.
Progress on the deal has been stalling for months.

expressions.usage: 대조적 성과를 while로 연결하여 불균형을 부각하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] continues to [verb] unevenly, [verb-ing] at [A] while [verb-ing] on [B]
patterns.explanation: 불균등한 발전을 두 가지 대조적 분사로 구체화하는 구조
examples: The reform continues to progress unevenly, succeeding in urban areas while failing in rural ones.
The economy continues to recover unevenly, booming in services while shrinking in manufacturing.

drillQuestions.question: The technology continues to advance ______, excelling at some tasks while stalling on others.

sentences.notes: excelling과 stalling이라는 반의어적 분사를 while로 연결하여 AI 기술의 불균등한 발전을 압축적으로 표현한다.

sentences.original: Nobody knows whether today's enormous infrastructure investments will yield returns or become expensive monuments to misplaced optimism.
sentences.translation: 오늘날의 막대한 인프라 투자가 수익을 낼 것인지, 아니면 잘못된 낙관주의의 값비싼 기념비가 될 것인지는 아무도 모른다.

grammarElements.text: whether today's enormous infrastructure investments will yield returns or become expensive monuments to misplaced optimism
grammarElements.label: whether ... or 명사절 (목적어)

vocabulary.example: These investments may never yield the returns investors expect.
The abandoned skyscraper stands as a monument to corporate hubris.
Their confidence turned out to be misplaced as the project collapsed.

expressions.usage: 대규모 투자가 실패로 끝났을 때 그 잔해를 비유적으로 표현하는 문학적 표현

patterns.pattern: Nobody knows whether [investment] will yield returns or become [metaphor for failure]
patterns.explanation: 성공과 실패의 양 극단을 whether ... or로 병치하여 근본적 불확실성을 표현하는 패턴
examples: Nobody knows whether the space program will yield breakthroughs or become a colossal waste of resources.
Nobody knows whether the merger will yield synergies or become a cautionary tale of corporate overreach.

drillQuestions.question: Nobody knows whether the investments will ______ returns or become expensive monuments.

sentences.notes: expensive monuments to misplaced optimism은 기사 전체에서 가장 문학적인 표현. 거대한 실패를 '기념비'에 비유하는 강렬한 은유이다.

sentences.original: What is clear is that the struggle for control over artificial intelligence has become one of the most consequential business battles in modern history, with stakes that have few historical parallels.
sentences.translation: 분명한 것은 인공지능에 대한 통제권을 둘러싼 투쟁이 현대 역사에서 가장 중대한 기업 전투 중 하나가 되었으며, 그 이해관계는 역사적으로 유사한 전례가 거의 없다는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: that the struggle for control over artificial intelligence has become one of the most consequential business battles in modern history
grammarElements.label: 보어 (that 명사절)

grammarElements.text: with stakes that have few historical parallels
grammarElements.label: with + 관계절 (부가 설명)

vocabulary.example: This is the most consequential election in a generation.
The crisis has few parallels in peacetime history.
The stakes in this negotiation could not be higher.

expressions.usage: 불확실한 논의를 마무리하면서 확실한 한 가지를 선언하는 강조 구문

patterns.pattern: What is clear is that [subject] has become one of the most [adjective] [noun] in [scope], with [stakes] that have few [parallels]
patterns.explanation: 불확실성 속에서도 확실한 한 가지를 선언하며 기사를 마무리하는 클로징 패턴
examples: What is clear is that climate change has become one of the most urgent challenges of our time, with consequences that have few precedents.
What is clear is that the race for quantum computing has become one of the most significant technological contests in decades, with implications that extend well beyond the lab.

drillQuestions.question: The struggle has become one of the most ______ business battles in modern history.

sentences.notes: 기사의 마지막 문장. What is clear is that 구조가 전체 불확실성 논의 속에서 한 가지 확실한 결론을 선언하는 수사적 마무리 기법이다. few historical parallels는 역사적 유례가 거의 없는 사안의 중대성을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: the struggle for control over artificial intelligence
one of the most consequential business battles
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 중대한 기업 전투 중 하나

koreanAlignment.en: with stakes that have few historical parallels
koreanAlignment.ko: 역사적으로 유사한 전례가 거의 없는 이해관계를 가진
